macro_events.event_summary: Strategists warn Europe faces physical crude/refined product shortages within weeks as inventories deplete amid an Iran war and Strait of Hormuz disruption.
primary_drivers: Physical supply shortage in Europe lifting Brent and refined product cracks
Strait of Hormuz disruption removing Middle East barrels from seaborne market
Tanker tonne-mile demand surge as Europe sources from Atlantic Basin / US instead of Middle East

beneficiaries.rationale: Diamondback is a pure-play Permian crude producer with no Middle East exposure; every $10 Brent move flows directly to FCF, and US barrels become the marginal supply to Europe.
Large US onshore oil producer with high oil cut and low breakevens; direct beneficiary of Brent spike and US export pull as Europe scrambles for non-Mideast barrels.
Scorpio Tankers operates the largest modern LR/MR product tanker fleet; European refined product shortage forces long-haul gasoline/diesel imports from US Gulf and Asia, spiking product tanker rates.
Frontline's VLCC/Suezmax fleet benefits as Europe replaces short-haul Mideast crude with long-haul Atlantic Basin / US barrels, lifting tonne-miles and spot rates sharply.
US East Coast refiner (Delaware City, Paulsboro) can arb European product shortage by exporting gasoline/diesel; crack spreads on transatlantic flows widen materially.
Largest US Gulf Coast refiner with major export infrastructure; directly capitalizes on European diesel/gasoline shortage via record export margins.
Cheniere's US LNG cargoes become more valuable as European energy crisis broadens beyond oil and TTF gas prices likely co-spike on substitution demand.
Integrated major with European downstream footprint and global trading desk profits from dislocations; upstream Brent-linked production captures the price spike.

losers.rationale: Delta has the highest European exposure among US airlines (~20% of capacity transatlantic) and no fuel hedges; jet fuel spike plus European demand destruction is a direct earnings hit.
Southwest unhedged on fuel after letting hedge program lapse; pure domestic carrier still faces full jet crack spread blowout from European product shortage pulling US barrels.
United's large transatlantic and Middle East/India network exposes it to both fuel price spike and route disruption / demand collapse to affected regions.
Stellantis derives majority of revenue from Europe; physical fuel shortages crush new vehicle demand and disrupt European manufacturing logistics.
Polaris discretionary powersports (snowmobiles, ATVs) are gasoline-intensive consumer goods; fuel shortage panic and price spike directly suppresses demand.
Philips is Netherlands-headquartered with European manufacturing; physical fuel shortage disrupts operations and crushes European healthcare capex sentiment.
FedEx's European ground network and transatlantic air freight face fuel surcharge lag and physical diesel rationing risk in key EU hubs.

headlines: ‘This is bad’: Strategists see European oil shortages within weeks as inventories are depleted

macro_events.event_summary: China has refused to approve any H200 purchases from Nvidia after Trump's 25% restriction on chips sold to China, costing Nvidia an estimated $30B in lost revenue.
primary_drivers: Loss of Nvidia China data center revenue (~$30B)
Market share vacuum filled by domestic Chinese AI chip makers
Shift of AI compute demand to non-China hyperscaler buildouts and alternative suppliers

beneficiaries.rationale: Direct GPU competitor to Nvidia; if Nvidia loses $30B in China revenue, US/EU hyperscalers diversifying away from a politically-exposed sole supplier rotate incremental AI accelerator orders to MI300/MI350 line. Also benefits from peer-rotation as funds underweight NVDA.

Custom ASIC business (Google TPU, Meta MTIA) is the direct substitute when hyperscalers want to reduce Nvidia dependency; not exposed to China export controls in the same way. Benefits from rotation trade out of NVDA.

Server OEM revenue is Nvidia-GPU dependent; however, non-China AI server demand absorption combined with potential AMD/Broadcom system wins keeps backlog intact. Mixed but net beneficiary versus pure-NVDA-China names.

losers.rationale: Headline directly quantifies $30B revenue loss — roughly 15-20% of data center segment depending on timeframe. Zero H200 approvals means no China DC GPU revenue. Guidance reset risk into next print.

Nvidia is one of TSMC's largest CoWoS/N4 customers; $30B of canceled H200 demand reduces leading-edge wafer and advanced packaging utilization. Partial offset from AMD/Broadcom but net negative on volumes.
EUV tool demand is tied to leading-edge capacity expansion at TSMC for Nvidia; lost China AI demand reduces marginal fab buildout urgency.
Active electrical cable and SerDes business levered to Nvidia GB200/H200 rack deployments; lost China shipments reduce TAM for AECs.
Power and cooling for Nvidia AI racks; lost China H200 deployments translate to fewer rack-level liquid cooling and PDU orders. Partially offset by non-China AI capex.
Grace CPU paired with Hopper/Blackwell uses Arm IP; lost China H200 volumes mean lost royalty units.
HBM3E supplier into Nvidia H200; $30B of canceled H200 demand is a direct hit to HBM bit allocations, partially offset by AMD MI series HBM consumption.

headlines: Trump's 25% cut on Nvidia chips sold to China backfired — Beijing won't approve a single H200 purchase, costs Huang $30B

macro_events.event_summary: EU is cutting growth forecasts and raising inflation forecasts, citing a 'stagflationary shock' from the Iran war driving oil prices higher.
primary_drivers: Sustained higher crude oil prices from Middle East supply risk premium
European demand destruction and weaker EUR-exposed revenue
Stagflation = higher input costs + weaker consumer = margin compression for Europe-exposed discretionary

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated major with ~40% of earnings from upstream; direct beneficiary of sustained Brent premium from Iran war supply risk. US-listed, no Iranian exposure to lose.
Same upstream leverage to Brent as XOM; benefits directly from war risk premium in crude. EU stagflation confirms oil shock is persisting, not transient.
Pure-play E&P with highest beta to crude prices among large caps; no downstream offset, so earnings revisions move 1:1 with oil deck.
Levered US E&P (Permian); high operating leverage to sustained $90+ Brent, and Buffett-backing supports flows on the theme.
Cheniere directly benefits as EU scrambles for non-Russian, non-Mideast gas; stagflation in EU = political imperative to lock more US LNG offtake. EU exposure is the thesis, not a drag.

losers.rationale: Philip Morris derives ~100% of revenue from international markets with heavy EU exposure; EU growth downgrade + weaker EUR translation hits earnings directly.
AB InBev headquartered in Belgium with major Western European beer volumes; EU consumer slowdown + input cost (barley, energy) inflation is textbook stagflation margin squeeze.
Stellantis sells heavily into European auto market (Peugeot, Fiat, Opel); EU growth cut + higher fuel prices crushes new vehicle demand and pricing power.
LyondellBasell has large European petrochemical footprint; gets squeezed on both ends — higher naphtha/energy feedstock costs and weaker EU industrial demand.
Philips is Netherlands-based with majority EU/global revenue; consumer health + capex-sensitive medical equipment hit by EU stagflation.
Deutsche Bank earnings tied to German/EU economy; stagflation = weaker loan demand, rising credit costs, ECB caught between hiking into recession or tolerating inflation.
Ferrari Italy-domiciled, EUR-reporting; European luxury auto demand softens as EU consumer outlook deteriorates, and EUR weakness pressures ADR.

headlines: The EU will cut growth outlook, raise inflation forecast as Iran war drives 'stagflationary shock'

macro_events.event_summary: Bond market positioning and trade flows suggest expectations for significantly higher long-end Treasury yields ahead.
primary_drivers: Higher long-end yields raise discount rates on long-duration equity cash flows
Steeper curve and higher rates expand bank net interest margins
Higher mortgage rates compress housing affordability and REIT cap rates

beneficiaries.rationale: Charles Schwab earns substantial net interest income on client cash sweep balances; higher short and intermediate rates directly expand spread income and reduce the cash-sorting drag that hurt it in prior cycles.
Largest US bank with asset-sensitive balance sheet; rising long-end yields with a steeper curve boost NIM on loan repricing while deposit betas lag.
Life insurer with long-duration liabilities benefits from higher reinvestment yields on its fixed income portfolio, improving spread income and book value economics on new business.
P&C insurer with large float invested in fixed income; higher reinvestment yields compound earnings power as the portfolio rolls.

losers.rationale: Largest US homebuilder; higher mortgage rates from rising 10Y compress affordability, slow order growth, and force incentive spending that compresses margins.

Long-duration, high-multiple growth equity whose valuation is highly sensitive to discount rate; rising real yields compress forward P/E most acutely for unprofitable-near-term, high-terminal-value names.
Prologis — largest industrial REIT; REITs face dual headwind of higher cap rates pressuring NAV and rising debt refinancing costs as long rates climb.
Realty Income — bond-proxy net lease REIT with long-duration lease cash flows; trades inversely to 10Y yield given its dividend-substitute investor base.
Highly levered consumer finance/used-car platform; rising rates increase floorplan financing costs, hurt auto loan affordability for subprime buyers, and pressure its own refinancing.

headlines: Ominous bond trades point to much higher rates

macro_events.event_summary: The IMF said the Bank of England does not need to hike rates and may even need to cut, signaling a dovish UK monetary policy outlook.
primary_drivers: Lower expected UK discount rate / softer GBP
Improved outlook for UK rate-sensitive consumer and housing demand
Weaker GBP boosts USD-reporting UK exporters' translated earnings

beneficiaries.rationale: Barclays has large UK retail/mortgage book; a dovish BoE signal lowers funding stress and supports UK housing credit demand, even as NIM faces modest pressure — net positive for loan growth and asset quality in UK consumer book.
UK-listed pharma with USD revenue base; weaker GBP from dovish BoE outlook mechanically lifts GBP-translated earnings, and lower UK rates support long-duration pharma valuations.

headlines: Bank of England does not need to hike interest rates, says IMF — it may even need to cut
